personas.name: Dr. Carlos — Médico Intensivista
personas.context_2l: 12-year ICU intensivist, 20-bed adult ICU at Hospital AUSTA; splits time 60% procedures / 40% patient review.
personas.needs_2l: Wants real-time deterioration detection (not a nurse hand-calculating MEWS hours later), 24h score trend views, and high-specificity alerts — currently receives 200 alerts/day with 80% false positives.
personas.flow_2l: Arrives 7h, reviews critical patients, joins 8h multidisciplinary round; responds to deterioration alerts between procedures during the day and covers remote calls at night.

personas.name: Enf. Ana — Enfermeira de UTI
personas.context_2l: 8-year ICU nurse responsible for 4-5 patients per shift; today manually collects vitals and calculates scores.
personas.needs_2l: Wants automatic MEWS calculation (currently 20 min/shift per patient manually), clear identification of which parameter triggered deterioration (RR? SpO2? BP?), and fast logging of post-alert actions.
personas.flow_2l: 12h shift, collects vitals every 2h (more often if critical), manually calculates and charts MEWS, escalates to on-call physician if high, documents interventions.

personas.name: Dra. Fernanda — Coordenadora de UTI
personas.context_2l: ICU medical coordinator over 30 beds spanning adult, coronary, and neonatal ICUs; reports quality metrics to clinical direction.
personas.needs_2l: Wants real-time occupancy/acuity dashboard, average alert response time as a quality indicator, and data (not perception) to justify bed-capacity investment.
personas.flow_2l: Morning round with medical team; reviews quality indicators (mortality, response time, infection rate); meets with clinical direction/administration; manages bed allocation, discharges, admissions, transfers.

personas.name: Dr. Rafael — Equipe de Resposta Rápida (RRT)
personas.context_2l: Rapid response team physician answering acute-deterioration calls anywhere in the hospital; carries a corporate smartphone.
personas.needs_2l: Wants mobile alerts with score, trend, and patient location; latest vitals accessible while en route; fast post-encounter outcome documentation.
personas.flow_2l: Receives critical-score notification on smartphone, reviews patient data while moving to location, attends and stabilizes patient, documents outcome (improved, transferred to ICU, etc.).

facts.claim: PT: "Score disponível em <30s após coleta de vitals" — EN: Clinical score must be available in under 30 seconds after vitals collection. Quantified target: 30 s max latency.
PT: "Menos de 3 falsos positivos por paciente-dia" — EN: Fewer than 3 false-positive alerts per patient-day. Quantified target: <3 FP/patient-day.
PT: "Visualização rápida de tendências (gráfico 24h)" — EN: Fast visualization of trends via a 24-hour trend chart. Quantified target: 24 h trend window.
PT: "Zero cálculos manuais de score" — EN: Zero manual score calculations by the nurse. Quantified target: 0 manual calculations.
PT: "Alerta mostra exatamente qual parâmetro disparou" — EN: The alert must show exactly which parameter triggered it. No numeric target (qualitative UX requirement).
PT: "Registro rápido de resposta ao alerta (1 clique)" — EN: Fast alert-response logging in 1 click. Quantified target: 1 click.
PT: "Dashboard de ocupação em tempo real" — EN: Real-time occupancy dashboard. Quantified target: real-time (no fixed numeric latency stated).
PT: "Relatório semanal de KPIs clínicos" — EN: Weekly clinical KPI report. Quantified target: weekly (7-day) cadence.
PT: "Dados exportáveis para ferramentas de qualidade hospitalar" — EN: Data must be exportable to hospital quality-management tools. No numeric target (capability requirement).
PT: "Notificação mobile em <5 segundos após score crítico" — EN: Mobile notification within under 5 seconds after a critical score. Quantified target: 5 s max latency.
PT: "Tela de paciente com últimos vitals + tendência" — EN: Patient screen showing latest vitals plus trend. No numeric target (feature/content requirement).
PT: "Documentação de desfecho em <1 minuto" — EN: Outcome documentation completed in under 1 minute. Quantified target: 1 min max.

constraints.text: SLO: clinical score MUST be available in <30s after vitals collection (Dr. Carlos).
SLO: alerting MUST sustain <3 false positives per patient-day (Dr. Carlos).
Invariant: score calculation MUST be fully automated — zero manual score calculations required of nursing staff (Enf. Ana).
Policy: every alert MUST expose which specific parameter triggered it (Enf. Ana).
UX invariant: alert-response acknowledgment/logging MUST be completable in 1 click (Enf. Ana).
SLO: mobile deterioration notifications MUST reach the RRT physician in <5s after a critical score (Dr. Rafael).
SLO: outcome documentation flow MUST be completable in <1 minute (Dr. Rafael).
Policy: unit-level data MUST be exportable to external hospital quality-management tools (Dra. Fernanda).
